description_long: This object is a compact, clamshell-style handheld gaming console reminiscent of the Game Boy Advance SP, featuring a matte black exterior with striking red buttons and accents. The upper half consists of a rectangular display screen with visible scratches and a 'Gameboy' label at the top. The lower half houses a directional pad, four circular action buttons, and two rectangular buttons labeled 'ON OFF' and 'Volume' on the sides. The rear view reveals two shoulder buttons marked 'R' and 'L', as well as hinge mechanisms connecting the two halves. The device appears to be constructed primarily from plastic, with a likely internal metal frame and electronic components. Its size and design suggest it is easily portable and intended for handheld use in various urban environments.
description: A compact, black and red clamshell handheld gaming console with a scratched display, labeled buttons, and a portable design. The device features a matte finish and prominent physical controls.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open screen, labeled 'Gameboy', and the main control buttons including the D-pad and action buttons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile, hinge, and the 'ON OFF' switch on the left edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the screen, hinge, and the red 'R' and 'L' shoulder buttons.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the 'Volume' control and the arrangement of buttons from a different angle.
